tasks.task_name: Look up eyeglasses stores in Kyoto for early trip shopping
tasks.hook_line: Kyoto might have hidden optical shops near temples — could you knock out 2-3 pairs before even reaching Tokyo?
tasks.reward: Gets eyeglasses shopping front-loaded so the rest of the trip is pure exploration
nuggets: 4 pairs in Japan saves $400+ vs US
tasks.system_prompt: Help me find eyeglasses stores in Kyoto, Japan that cater to English-speaking tourists. I'm planning to buy 4 pairs of prescription glasses during my Japan trip (March 23 - April 7) and want to start shopping early in Kyoto (March 25-27). I need stores that can work with a US prescription, provide same-day or next-day service, and ideally are located near central Kyoto hotels or major temple districts. Provide store names, addresses, Google Maps links, price ranges, and any reviews about English service.

tasks.task_name: Validate insurance/prescription requirements for buying 4 pairs of glasses
tasks.first_domino: Email insurance: 'Subject: International eyeglasses coverage Japan'
tasks.hook_line: Leaving in 3 days — need to confirm insurance won't reject claims for international glasses purchases
tasks.reward: Eliminates the risk of spending $800+ out-of-pocket with no reimbursement
nuggets: US insurance may not cover international
tasks.system_prompt: Help me figure out the insurance and prescription requirements for buying 4 pairs of eyeglasses in Japan. I have US health insurance and need to know: (1) Will my insurance cover international optical purchases? (2) What documentation do I need to bring (prescription format, PD measurement, etc.)? (3) Can I get reimbursed if I pay out-of-pocket in Japan? (4) What's the typical process for filing claims for international eyeglasses? I'm leaving for Japan in 3 days (March 23) and want to buy glasses in Kyoto, Takayama, and Tokyo.

tasks.first_domino: Open Google Maps and search 'Kyoto temples gardens'
tasks.hook_line: Kyoto has 2000+ temples — which ones are actually worth the early wake-up?
tasks.reward: Closes out Kyoto planning — only Takayama and Tokyo left
nuggets: Fushimi Inari free, open 24hrs, dawn best
tasks.system_prompt: Help me plan activities for 2.5 days in Kyoto (March 25-27, 2026). Traveling with my girlfriend Dana. We like temples, walking, nature, quiet outdoor spaces, and good food. Already have hotels booked in central Kyoto. I want a day-by-day itinerary with opening hours, entry fees, Google Maps links, and suggested walking routes. Include a mix of major landmarks (Fushimi Inari, Kinkaku-ji, Arashiyama) and less touristy spots. Also suggest good restaurants near each day's route. Optimize for avoiding crowds — we're willing to wake up early.

tasks.hook_line: Takayama is a mountain town — are there hidden trails or onsen worth adding to the route?
tasks.reward: Unlocks the middle leg of the trip — Takayama becomes more than just a stopover
nuggets: Takayama old town free walking area
tasks.system_prompt: Help me plan activities for 2-3 days in Takayama, Japan (late March 2026). Traveling with my girlfriend Dana. We like nature, hiking, quiet mountain towns, and outdoor spaces. Takayama is between Kyoto and Tokyo on our route. I want suggestions for day trips (Shirakawa-go?), local hikes, onsen, sake breweries, and the old town. Include opening hours, transportation options, Google Maps links, and whether any activities need advance booking. We don't need intense hikes — scenic walks and cultural experiences are great.

tasks.first_domino: Google 'Tokyo neighborhoods what to see 4 days'
tasks.hook_line: Tokyo is massive — 4 days means picking neighborhoods wisely instead of trying to see everything
tasks.reward: Completes all activity planning — the trip becomes fully mapped out
nuggets: Limited edition fountain pen shops in Ginza
tasks.system_prompt: Help me plan activities for 4 days in Tokyo (early April 2026). Traveling with my girlfriend Dana. We like walking, food, quiet neighborhoods, parks, temples, and unique shops. I'm also shopping for fountain pens (limited editions in Ginza) and eyeglasses. I want a day-by-day itinerary organized by neighborhood to minimize transit. Include opening hours, Google Maps links, restaurant suggestions, and fountain pen shop recommendations. Mix major landmarks (Senso-ji, Meiji Shrine) with less touristy neighborhoods. We prefer avoiding crowds when possible.

tasks.first_domino: Clone the Cerebro repo and open in VS Code
tasks.hook_line: Getting a developer portal running locally is the classic 'battle with dependencies' puzzle
tasks.reward: Unblocks all future Cerebro work — can finally start contributing instead of just reading about it
nuggets: May need Docker or local services
tasks.system_prompt: Help me get the Cerebro developer portal running locally on my machine. Cerebro is an internal work project (developer portal for engineers). I haven't worked on it since February and need to pick it back up. Walk me through: (1) What dependencies I'll likely need (Node, Docker, databases, etc.), (2) Common setup issues for developer portals, (3) How to verify it's running correctly, (4) What documentation to check first. Assume I'm starting fresh — I may have cloned it before but don't remember the setup steps.

recommended_slots.reason: Top-priority travel prep leaving today — quick research task perfect for ramp_up energy before work hours
Quick coordination errand that unblocks glasses shopping — low effort, medium-high urgency
Medium-effort research that builds momentum — can batch with other Japan planning if energy holds
